mechanism_narrative: Sustained improvement in nonfarm payrolls signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as employment gains translate into consumption and investment growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=142373 in Oct 2020, rising from April low
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative or zero in more than 40% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% from its pandemic peak signals that the labor market is healing sufficiently to sustain consumer spending and risk appetite, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.9 in Oct 2020, down from 14.8% in April
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 6.5%, the next 60-day SPX return is negative or the average return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p>0.05) over all occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero short-term rates and rising housing starts indicates that monetary accommodation is flowing into the real economy via housing investment, which boosts construction employment, durable goods demand, and financial sector profits, pushing S&P 500 higher.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 in Oct 2020, HOUST=1415 in Sep 2020
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and HOUST is above 1400, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than one-third of such joint regimes, or the rank correlation between the product (FEDFUNDS*HOUST) and returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In the early recovery phase characterized by high unemployment and extremely low interest rates, strong retail sales reflect pent-up consumer demand fueled by fiscal transfers and confidence, which immediately lifts equity valuations as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=549256 in Sep 2020, significantly above pre-pandemic levels
regime_hint: early recovery with elevated unemployment and near-zero rates
failure_condition: Within the regime where UNRATE>6% and FEDFUNDS<0.25%, month-over-month increases in RSAFS do not predict positive 5-day SPX returns with at least 60% accuracy over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals that manufacturing is recovering from pandemic disruptions, increasing capacity utilization and corporate profits, which lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as supply chains normalize.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.5055 in Sep 2020, up from April trough
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the long-short spread is negative.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from its effective zero bound would signal the end of extraordinary accommodation, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns in the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.09, below any tightening threshold
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.15%, the average 20-day SPX return is positive, or the frequency of negative returns is below 55% over all historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises while short-term rates remain near zero, real interest rates become deeply negative, incentivizing investors to shift from cash and bonds into equities as an inflation hedge, pushing S&P 500 prices upward.
regime_hint: low nominal rate environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below CPIAUCSL inflation (negative real rates), the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the rank correlation of (CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS) with returns is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero interest rate regime, strong housing starts act as a leading indicator for broader economic expansion—spurring construction jobs, household wealth, and durable goods demand—which boosts S&P 500 forward returns as risk appetite remains high.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1415 in Sep 2020, near cycle high
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.10%, the correlation between HOUST month-over-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative or less than 0.10 over a 2-year period.
